finding_results.notes: Replaced the telling narration at the water-delivery moment ('The old Derry would have been talking already, would have been narrating the whole thing back to him in his looping, circular way...') with an actual Derry line in his established register: run-on syntax, domestic deflection to Pell's fever, trailing conjunction cut off mid-clause. The line shows Derry's natural voice leaking through his enforced silence — he reflexively starts a Pell story while giving water-drinking advice, then catches himself and shuts down. His withdrawal is now visibly active (mouth closing over the rest of the sentence, standing and leaving) rather than generic muteness. The Pell mention also gains dramatic charge from the earlier narration establishing that Derry hasn't said her name since before Caul.

summary: Single finding addressed. Gave Derry a pressured line in his own idiolect at the water-delivery scene (line 207), replacing narrator exposition about what his voice used to sound like. The line uses his established run-on syntax and Pell-deflection habit, then shows him actively shutting it down. Minimal collateral change — surrounding narration trimmed only where it duplicated what the new dialogue now demonstrates.
